violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener in kitchen and slicer in culinary kitchen) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 11/05/2025

Foods at the salad bar, located in the self-service area, are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the (QT) sanitizing solution for the (3-bay sinks). New Violation. Correct By: 11/05/2025
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine in the (kitchen). New Violation. Correct By: 11/05/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following: -shelves of underneath cook line -soda fountains in front area -in kitchen area behind and underneath equipment have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 11/05/2025

The pre-flush spray hose at the 3 bay sink in the culinary kitchen is hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2025
Some ceiling tiles in the dough room, located in the culinary kitchen area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2025

Observed establishment lacked clean up kit for vomiting and diarrhea. Procedures for vomiting and diarrhea printed and reviewed with PIC. English Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils in the main kitchen (can opener and mixer) and in culinary kitchen (slicer and mixer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC took equipment above to be washed, rinsed, and sanitized during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2025

Chicken stock (prep 1/26/25) was observed marked with the date that extended the 7 days for TCS foods. PIC voluntary disposed of chicken stock. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2025

ROP Salmon observed thawing with package intact in walk-in cooler (label on the ROP salmon package states food item is to remain frozen until used). Salmon was observed at 38 degrees F. Proper thawing procedures for ROP products were reviewed. Staffed removed ROP salmon from the package during the time for inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2025

Foods at the salad bar, located in the self-service area, are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2025

The cutting boards in the culinary kitchen and main kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2025

The pre-flush spray hose at the 3 bay sink in the culinary kitchen is hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2025
Some ceiling tiles in the dough room, located in the culinary kitchen area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2025
Establishment could not provide proof of food safety manager present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2024

An employee was observed eating while handling food in the front service area. An EMPLOYEE shall eat, drink, or use any form of tobacco only in designated areas where the contamination of exposed FOOD; clean EQUIPMENT, UTENSILS, and LINENS; unwrapped SINGLE-SERVICE and SINGLE-USE ARTICLES; or other items needing protection can not result. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2024
The hand-washing sink near the front service line was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2024

Raw animal foods (such as eggs and liquid eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as vegetables and fruits) in a refrigeration unit and the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2024

Several foods (see temperatures) were observed held at 46-50 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food had just recently been prepared but then stored outside of temperature control. Inspector instructed PIC to place foods in ice water bath for service but that a permanant refrigeration solution must be in place moving forward. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2024

Reduced oxygen packaged seafood was observed thawing with the packaging intact. ROP seafood must be removed from its reduced oxygen state to allow for adequate oxygen transfer rate. PIC volunatrily disposed of food at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2024

Apples were observed held out in front service area without protection from consumer contamination. Except for nuts in the shell and whole, raw fruits and vegetables that are intended for hulling, peeling, or washing by the CONSUMER before consumption, FOOD on display shall be protected from contamination by the use of PACKAGING; counter, service line, or salad bar FOOD guards; display cases; or other effective means. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2024
The facility is lacking adequate test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the three bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the large mixers in both the main kitchen and culinary classroom kitchen were observed with an accumulation of food debris and other residue. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

The condensate pipe from the fan system in the walk-in cooler (located in the culinary class area) was observed with a leak. A bucket was observed being used to catch leaking water. This system must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as cooked pasta) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/26/2023

Various items (see disposal form) were marked with a date exceeding 7 days. Items prepared/prepped/cooked in the establishment shall be used within 7 days. PIC voluntarily disposed of food items at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/26/2023
Per PIC, multi-use surface cleaner is being used for sanitizing the slicer. The multi-use surface cleaner is to not be used for food contact surfaces. New Violation. Correct By: 10/26/2023

Apples were observed in front area available for consumers without protection from contamination. Except for nuts in the shell and whole, raw fruits and vegetables that are intended for hulling, peeling, or washing by the CONSUMER before consumption, FOOD on display shall be protected from contamination by the use of PACKAGING; counter, service line, or salad bar FOOD guards; display cases; or other effective means. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/26/2023

The following was observed in poor repair: 1) A knife in the cafeteria side was observed to be chipped 2) The sanitizer dispenser at the 3-bay sink in the cafeteria side observed to not be pulling sanitizer New Violation. Correct By: 11/02/2023
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine in the cafeteria side. New Violation. Correct By: 11/02/2023
